title: lophiaris lurida
intro: Lophiaris lurida, also known as Desert Marigold or Fendler's False Aster, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to be effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lophiaris lurida include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be dried and used as a poultice to apply directly to affected areas.
